context: Context: Campus and Workplace Small Groups (Philippines)
icebreaker_text: Have everyone check their phone contacts or FB friends. Find one person you "know" but haven't actually talked to in over a year. Why do we say we "know" people when we only see their curated posts?
big_idea: Real life isn't just about knowing facts about God, but actually being BFFs with Him.
key_verse: And this is eternal life, that they know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ whom you have sent.
section1_content:
The Bible isn't just an old history book; it is actually the clearest window we have into God’s heart. From the very first page of Genesis to the final shout in Revelation, God is trying to peel back the curtain between our messy world and His invisible kingdom. He wants us to see that He isn’t some distant force or a grumpy judge, but a God who is all-powerful yet incredibly kind. While we might feel like God is a mystery we can never solve, He actually went out of His way to show up in person through Jesus. By looking at Jesus, we see God’s justice, His mercy, and His crazy-strong power over death all wrapped into one. Knowing Him this way changes how we see our origin and our future because we finally realize who is really in control. We are invited to study His character not to pass a test, but to fall in love with the One who made us. This journey of learning about Him never really ends; even in heaven, we will keep discovering new layers of His goodness. Jeremiah 32:17 reminds us that nothing is too hard for the God who made the heavens and the earth.

Think about how you feel when you finally meet a celebrity or an influencer you’ve followed for years. You might know their favorite food or their OOTD, but you don't actually know them until you sit down for a meal and hear their voice. Many of us treat God like a celebrity we follow from a distance, scrolling through "Bible verses of the day" without ever having a real conversation. We have all the stats—we know He’s powerful and "all-knowing"—but it feels like head knowledge rather than a heart connection. It’s like having a 5-star rating for a restaurant you’ve never actually eaten at. We talk about His grace and forgiveness as concepts, but we rarely let those things change our actual mood on a stressful Monday morning. To truly know someone, you have to spend time in their presence, not just read their bio.

This week, try to move from "following" God to actually "friending" Him by spending intentional time in His Word. Don't just read the Bible to finish a checklist; read it to find a detail about God’s personality that you never noticed before. Maybe you’ll notice how patient He was with His stubborn followers or how He prioritized the lonely people in a crowd. When you see a specific trait—like His mercy—write it down and thank Him for being that way toward you specifically. As you learn more, your love for Him will naturally deepen, and you’ll find yourself wanting to be more like Him. You don't have to understand everything today; just start with the "truest, clearest picture" we have in the Gospels. Let the light of His goodness chase away the confusing "darkness" of who you thought He was.
section1_question:
Para sa iyo, ano ang biggest difference between knowing about God and actually knowing Him personally?

In your daily student or work life, which of God's traits (mercy, justice, power) do you need to experience the most right now?
Follow-up: "How do you think answering with openness and honesty would change that conversation?"
section1_key_truth: "The Lord is righteous in all his ways and kind in all his works." (Psalm 145:17, ESV)
section2_content:
Since the very beginning in the Garden of Eden, there has been a massive "PR war" against God’s reputation. Lucifer was the first one to tweet out fake news about God’s character, suggesting that He was hiding the good stuff from us. Satan doesn't care if you are religious or if you believe in some "higher power," as long as your picture of God is distorted. If the enemy can make you believe that God is a micromanager, a killjoy, or someone who doesn't have your best interest at heart, he has already won. The whole "Great Controversy" or the big battle between good and evil is actually centered on this one question: Is God actually good? When we doubt His promises, we are essentially entertaining the same lies that Eve heard under the tree. Every temptation we face is rooted in the idea that God is holding out on us and that we need to take matters into our own hands. Genesis 3:1-5 shows us exactly how the enemy twists God's words to make Him look like the villain.

Imagine you have a group chat where one person is constantly "lowkey" throwing shade at a friend who isn't there. They spread rumors that the friend is actually selfish or that they are talking behind your back, even though that friend has always been loyal to you. Eventually, you start to feel awkward around that friend, not because they changed, but because your perception of them was poisoned. This is exactly what happens in our spiritual lives when we listen to the "shade" the world throws at God. We start to view His laws as heavy burdens rather than protective boundaries because we've been told He’s a control freak. We see trials as proof that He doesn't care, rather than opportunities to see His power at work. The world tells us that "living your truth" is better than following a God who "keeps secrets."

We need to be "fact-checkers" when it comes to the thoughts we have about God’s character. When a thought enters your mind saying, "God is disappointed in you" or "God won't help you this time," ask yourself if that aligns with the Bible. If it doesn't match the "consistent picture" of God found in the life of Jesus, then it is a lie from the enemy. We have to decide every single day who we are going to believe: the accusations of the enemy or the promises of the Father. Choosing to trust God’s goodness, even when things are going wrong, is the ultimate way to win this battle. By focusing on His truth, we shed light into the darkness of our own misunderstandings. Don't let the enemy besmirch the reputation of the One who literally died to be close to you.
section2_question:
Ano yung common "fake news" or misconceptions about God na naririnig mo sa mga classmates or coworkers mo?

How can we "fact-check" our negative feelings about God using what we read in the Bible?
section2_key_truth: "As iron sharpens iron, so one person sharpens another." (Proverbs 27:17, ESV)
section3_content:
The final message God wants to send to a dark and confused world is a clear revelation of His love. As we get closer to the end of history, the "darkness of misapprehension" is getting thicker, and people are losing their grip on who God really is. God’s response to this isn't a loud argument or a display of raw anger, but a "message illuminating in its influence." He wants His character—His goodness, mercy, and truth—to be seen through the lives of people who actually know Him. When we reflect His love, we become like small lights in a very dark room, showing others the way home. This isn't just about being "nice"; it's about being a living testimony of the saving power of God's character. The goal of discipleship is to reflect His image so clearly that when people see us, they get a better idea of what He is like. John 17:23 says that when we are united in His love, the world will finally know that God sent Jesus and loves them too.

Have you ever noticed how people who spend a lot of time together start to talk and act the same way? Best friends often pick up each other's slang, and couples sometimes even start to look alike because their expressions mirror one another. This "mirror effect" is supposed to happen in our relationship with God, too. If we are constantly hanging out with Him through prayer and His Word, His "vibe" should start rubbing off on us. If we say we follow a merciful God but we are the first ones to "cancel" someone or hold a grudge at work, our "reflection" is blurry. People around us on campus or in the office might never pick up a Bible, but they are "reading" our lives every day. We are the only "Jesus" some people will ever meet.

Make it your mission to be a "revelation of His character" in your specific circle this week. When you are tempted to be impatient with a slow groupmate or a demanding boss, remember how patient God is with you. When you have the chance to show mercy to someone who messed up, do it as a way of pointing them toward the God of second chances. Our "action" speaks much louder than any religious jargon we could use. As we reflect His love, we aren't just being "good people"—we are participating in the "last message of mercy" to the world. Let the light of His glory shine through your kindness, your honesty, and your peace. You don't have to be perfect; you just have to be a clear mirror reflecting the Perfect One.
section3_question:
In what specific situation this week can you show "mercy" or "truth" to someone who doesn't deserve it?

Bakit minsan mahirap mag-reflect ng character ni God sa workplace o school, and how can we ask Him for help?
section3_key_truth: "Therefore, as God's chosen people, holy and dearly loved, clothe yourselves with compassion, kindness, humility, gentleness and patience." (Colossians 3:12, ESV)
key_insight: As you go through your week, remember that God isn't hiding from you. He is the Father running down the road to meet you, the Shepherd looking for the lost sheep, and the Friend who sticks closer than a brother. Even when you feel like you’re in the dark, His character remains constant—He is always good, always just, and always loving. Don't let the noise of the world drown out the truth of His Word. The more you look at Jesus, the more the shadows of doubt will disappear. You were made to know Him, and in knowing Him, you will find exactly who you were meant to be. There is so much hope in the fact that the Creator of the universe wants a personal relationship with you.
action_step: Choose one "attribute" of God we discussed (like Mercy or Patience). Identify one person at your school or work who is currently "difficult" to deal with, and intentionally show them that specific attribute of God today through a simple act of kindness or a supportive message.
struggles_list:
Felt like a failure
Made a big mistake
Drifted from prayer/Bible reading
Hurt someone I care about
Gave in to temptation
Doubted God's goodness
safespace_list:
Listen without interrupting
No gossip rule
Share our own struggles first
Pray for each other regularly
Check in during the week
Celebrate small wins together
